hierarchyType.description: Optional. Links this leaf to a ComplaintHierarchyDefinition. Absent => legacy flat menuPath grouping (unchanged behaviour).

authorityType.description: Optional denormalised ancestor (top level). Canonical source is the leaf's ClassificationNode path; recomputed on load, never hand-edited.

sector.description: Optional denormalised ancestor (sector level). Mirrors menuPath for back-compat.

path.description: Optional dot-delimited materialised path of the leaf's ancestor chain, e.g. IGE.IGE_COMPLAINT.HEALTH.

parentCode.description: Optional code of the immediate parent ClassificationNode (the sector node).

description: Per-tenant auto-escalation config consumed by pgr-services EscalationScheduler. `defaultSlaByLevel` is an array of SLA windows in MILLISECONDS, one per escalation depth (level 0 = first scan); `overrides` keys by serviceCode for per-complaint-type SLA arrays; `maxDepth` caps how many times a single complaint can be auto-escalated before stopping. Per-level enablement is opt-in via `enabledByLevel` (boolean array, same length as `defaultSlaByLevel`; defaults to all-true when omitted) and `overrides[<serviceCode>].enabledByLevel` for per-service-code overrides. Without an EscalationConfig record pgr-services falls back to its 5-day default SLA — see https://github.com/egovernments/Citizen-Complaint-Resolution-System/issues/585.

enabledByLevel.description: Optional per-level enablement flags. Same length as defaultSlaByLevel. Missing or empty = all levels enabled (current behaviour). Per @vinothrallapalli-eGov review on PR #692 — keeps the door open for tenants that want escalation only at level N without rewriting the schema.

overrides.description: Per-serviceCode SLA override. Value may be EITHER (a) a plain array of SLA windows (same shape as defaultSlaByLevel, applies to all levels) for backward compatibility, OR (b) an object with explicit `slaByLevel` and optional `enabledByLevel` for per-service-code per-level control.

description: Declares the ORDERED, CONFIGURABLE levels of a complaint-type hierarchy for a tenant. Direct analogue of boundary-service's HierarchyDefinition. One record per (tenantId, hierarchyType). The number/identity of levels is pure data in `levels[]` — add/remove an element to change depth, no schema or code change. A tenant with NO record falls back to legacy flat menuPath grouping.

hierarchyType.description: Short uppercase identifier for this hierarchy, e.g. PGR.

levels.description: Ordered top->leaf levels. Exactly one level should set isLeafServiceCode=true (its nodes' codes are ServiceDefs serviceCodes).

description: Adjacency-list node for the NON-LEAF levels of a complaint hierarchy (boundary entity + relationship, collapsed into one MDMS row because complaint nodes carry no geometry). `levelCode` matches a levels[].levelCode; `parentCode` points at the parent node's code; `path` is the dot-delimited materialised path computed parent-before-child by the loader. Leaf (serviceCode) nodes are NOT stored here — the leaf is the ServiceDefs record itself.
Per-module visible window into a complaint hierarchy (analogue of CMS-BOUNDARY.HierarchySchema). Bounds the dropdown window by naming the highest and lowest visible levels.

Maps a complaint sub-type (serviceCode) to MANY departments. `primaryDepartment` is the single value that ServiceDefs.department continues to mirror (preserves single-valued backend invariants); `departments[]` is the new many-list consumed only by new assignee-routing code.
